shortAnswer: Fasting blood glucose measures blood sugar concentration after at least 8 hours without eating. Values above 126 mg/dl may indicate diabetes, values between 100 and 125 mg/dl suggest prediabetes. Discuss elevated values with your doctor.
metaDescription: Fasting blood glucose elevated? Normal ranges, diabetes risk, and what the value means — explained simply.
doctorQuestions: Should I have an oral glucose tolerance test?
Do I have diabetes or prediabetes?
What dietary changes would help me?
pageTitle: Blood glucose value — Fasting blood sugar explained | Medyra
